data.content:
### GitHub Actions Workflow for Playwright Tests

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci-intro.md

These YAML configurations define GitHub Actions workflows to automate Playwright test execution for Python, Java, and C# projects. They trigger on push and pull requests, setting up the language environment, installing Playwright, and running tests on an Ubuntu runner.

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v6
      with:
        python-version: '3.13'
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt
    - name: Ensure browsers are installed
      run: python -m playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Run your tests
      run: pytest --tracing=retain-on-failure
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-traces
        path: test-results/
```

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-java@v5
      with:
        distribution: 'temurin'
        java-version: '25'
    - name: Build & Install
      run: mvn -B install -D skipTests --no-transfer-progress
    - name: Ensure browsers are installed
      run: mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="install --with-deps"
    - name: Run tests
      run: mvn test
```

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - name: Setup dotnet
      uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
      with:
        dotnet-version: 8.0.x
    - name: Build & Install
      run: dotnet build
    - name: Ensure browsers are installed
      run: pwsh bin/Debug/net8.0/playwright.ps1 install --with-deps
    - name: Run your tests
      run: dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Actions workflow for Playwright tests (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci-intro.md

This GitHub Actions workflow defines a CI/CD pipeline for JavaScript-based Playwright tests. It automatically runs tests on `push` and `pull_request` events to `main` or `master` branches, handles dependency installation, Playwright browser setup, test execution, and uploads the Playwright HTML report as a build artifact.

```yml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: playwright-report/
        retention-days: 30
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Playwright GitHub Actions Reporter (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-reporters-js.md

This snippet demonstrates how to conditionally enable the built-in 'github' reporter in Playwright's configuration file for GitHub Actions CI environments, falling back to a 'list' reporter for local runs.

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';

export default defineConfig({
  // 'github' for GitHub Actions CI to generate annotations, plus a concise 'dot'
  // default 'list' when running locally
  reporter: process.env.CI ? 'github' : 'list',
});
```

--------------------------------

### Upload Playwright HTML Report to Azure Storage in GitHub Actions Workflow

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci-intro.md

GitHub Actions workflow step that uploads Playwright HTML reports to Azure Storage static website using azcopy. Uses service principal authentication with secrets for secure access. Creates unique report directories based on GitHub run ID and attempt number, and outputs the public URL for accessing the report.

```yaml
...
    - name: Upload HTML report to Azure
      shell: bash
      run: |
        REPORT_DIR='run-${{ github.run_id }}-${{ github.run_attempt }}'
        azcopy cp --recursive "./playwright-report/*" "https://<STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME>.blob.core.windows.net/\$web/$REPORT_DIR"
        echo "::notice title=HTML report url::https://<STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME>.z1.web.core.windows.net/$REPORT_DIR/index.html"
      env:
        AZCOPY_AUTO_LOGIN_TYPE: SPN
        AZCOPY_SPA_APPLICATION_ID: '${{ secrets.AZCOPY_SPA_APPLICATION_ID }}'
        AZCOPY_SPA_CLIENT_SECRET: '${{ secrets.AZCOPY_SPA_CLIENT_SECRET }}'
        AZCOPY_TENANT_ID: '${{ secrets.AZCOPY_TENANT_ID }}'
```

--------------------------------

### Create Azure Service Principal for GitHub Actions with Storage Blob Access

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci-intro.md

Creates a service principal in Azure with Storage Blob Data Contributor role for automated report uploads. This command generates credentials needed for GitHub Actions secrets. Replace placeholders with your actual Azure subscription ID, resource group name, and storage account name.

```bash
az ad sp create-for-rbac --name "github-actions" --role "Storage Blob Data Contributor" --scopes /subscriptions/<SUBSCRIPTION_ID>/resourceGroups/<RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME>/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/<STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME>
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow for Playwright Tests

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

This YAML workflow defines a comprehensive GitHub Actions job designed to automate Playwright test execution upon `push` or `pull_request` events to `main` or `master` branches. It includes steps for repository checkout, Node.js setup, dependency installation, Playwright browser setup, test execution, and artifact upload for the HTML report.

```yml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright Browsers
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-report
        path: playwright-report/
        retention-days: 30
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions TypeScript Type Checking and Playwright Tests

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-typescript-js.md

YAML workflow configuration for running TypeScript type checks with tsc before executing Playwright tests in GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Ensures type safety without blocking test execution on non-critical compilation errors.

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: Run type checks
      run: npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit
    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Actions to Run Playwright Tests in a Container

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

These YAML workflows define GitHub Actions for running Playwright tests inside a Docker container using a language-specific Playwright image. Each example covers checking out the repository, setting up the respective language environment (Node.js, Python, Java, .NET), installing dependencies, and executing the tests. The container provides a consistent environment for visual regression and screenshot testing.

```yml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright:
    name: 'Playwright Tests'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v%%VERSION%%-noble
      options: --user 1001
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: lts/*
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Run your tests
        run: npx playwright test
```

```yml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright:
    name: 'Playwright Tests'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/python:v%%VERSION%%-noble
      options: --user 1001
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r local-requirements.txt
          pip install -e .
      - name: Run your tests
        run: pytest
```

```yml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright:
    name: 'Playwright Tests'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/java:v%%VERSION%%-noble
      options: --user 1001
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v5
        with:
          distribution: 'temurin'
          java-version: '25'
      - name: Build & Install
        run: mvn -B install -D skipTests --no-transfer-progress
      - name: Run tests
        run: mvn test
```

```yml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright:
    name: 'Playwright Tests'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/dotnet:v%%VERSION%%-noble
      options: --user 1001
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
      - name: Setup dotnet
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
        with:
          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
      - run: dotnet build
      - name: Run your tests
        run: dotnet test
```

### Configure Playwright Tests on Push/Pull Request with GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/ci.md

This GitHub Actions workflow sets up Playwright tests to run automatically on `push` or `pull_request` events to `main` or `master` branches. It checks out the repository, sets up the respective language environment (Python, Java, or C#), installs dependencies, ensures Playwright browsers are installed, and then executes the tests. For Python, it includes a step to upload Playwright traces as an artifact.

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v6
      with:
        python-version: '3.13'
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt
    - name: Ensure browsers are installed
      run: python -m playwright install --with-deps
    - name: Run your tests
      run: pytest --tracing=retain-on-failure
    - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v5
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      with:
        name: playwright-traces
        path: test-results/
```

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-java@v5
      with:
        distribution: 'temurin'
        java-version: '25'
    - name: Build & Install
      run: mvn -B install -D skipTests --no-transfer-progress
    - name: Ensure browsers are installed
      run: mvn exec:java -e -D exec.mainClass=com.microsoft.playwright.CLI -D exec.args="install --with-deps"
    - name: Run tests
      run: mvn test
```

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  test:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - name: Setup dotnet
      uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
      with:
        dotnet-version: 8.0.x
    - name: Build & Install
      run: dotnet build
    - name: Ensure browsers are installed
      run: pwsh bin/Debug/net8.0/playwright.ps1 install --with-deps
    - name: Run your tests
      run: dotnet test
```

--------------------------------

### Merge Playwright blob reports into single HTML report in GitHub Actions

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Configures a dependent GitHub Actions job that runs after all test shards complete, downloading all blob report artifacts and merging them into a consolidated HTML report. The job executes even if some shards fail, and uploads the final HTML report with a 14-day retention period.

```yaml
jobs:
...
  merge-reports:
    # Merge reports after playwright-tests, even if some shards have failed
    if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
    needs: [playwright-tests]

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci

    - name: Download blob reports from GitHub Actions Artifacts
      uses: actions/download-artifact@v5
      with:
        path: all-blob-reports
        pattern: blob-report-*
        merge-multiple: true

    - name: Merge into HTML Report
      run: npx playwright merge-reports --reporter html ./all-blob-reports

    - name: Upload HTML report
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: html-report--attempt-${{ github.run_attempt }}
        path: playwright-report
        retention-days: 14
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Actions workflow for parallel Playwright test sharding

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/test-sharding-js.md

Sets up a GitHub Actions job that runs Playwright tests in parallel across 4 shards using matrix strategy. Each shard executes a portion of the test suite and uploads its blob report as an artifact for later merging. The workflow triggers on push and pull request events to main/master branches.

```yaml
name: Playwright Tests
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, master ]
jobs:
  playwright-tests:
    timeout-minutes: 60
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        shardIndex: [1, 2, 3, 4]
        shardTotal: [4]
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v5
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
      with:
        node-version: lts/*
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Install Playwright browsers
      run: npx playwright install --with-deps

    - name: Run Playwright tests
      run: npx playwright test --shard=${{ matrix.shardIndex }}/${{ matrix.shardTotal }}

    - name: Upload blob report to GitHub Actions Artifacts
      if: ${{ !cancelled() }}
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: blob-report-${{ matrix.shardIndex }}
        path: blob-report
        retention-days: 1
```

--------------------------------

### Wait for Event - Action (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/api/params.md

Specify an action (Func<Task>) that performs the action triggering the event. C#-specific parameter.

```APIDOC
## Wait for Event Action (C#)

### Description
Action that triggers the event. Available in: C#.

### Parameters
- **action** (Func<Task>) - Required - Asynchronous action to execute that triggers the event.

### Request Example
```csharp
async () => await page.ClickAsync("button")
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create GitHub Issue via API and Verify on Page (C#)

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/api-testing-csharp.md

This test creates a new GitHub issue using the API and then navigates to the issues list page to verify that the newly created issue appears at the top. It utilizes Playwright's Request API for the POST request and LocatorAssertions for verification on the page. Dependencies include the Playwright testing framework and a configured GitHub repository.

```csharp
class TestGitHubAPI : PageTest
{
    [TestMethod]
    public async Task LastCreatedIssueShouldBeFirstInTheList()
    {
        var data = new Dictionary<string, string>
        {
            { "title", "[Feature] request 1" },
            { "body", "Feature description" }
        };
        var newIssue = await Request.PostAsync("/repos/" + USER + "/" + REPO + "/issues", new() { DataObject = data });
        await Expect(newIssue).ToBeOKAsync();

        // When inheriting from 'PlaywrightTest' it only gives you a Playwright instance. To get a Page instance, either start
        // a browser, context, and page manually or inherit from 'PageTest' which will launch it for you.
        await Page.GotoAsync("https://github.com/" + USER + "/" + REPO + "/issues");
        var firstIssue = Page.Locator("a[data-hovercard-type='issue']").First;
        await Expect(firstIssue).ToHaveTextAsync("[Feature] request 1");
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Verify GitHub Issues with Playwright Java API

Source: https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/main/docs/src/api-testing-java.md

This code demonstrates how to create new GitHub issues (bug reports and feature requests) using Playwright's API testing capabilities in Java. It posts issue data to the GitHub API, then retrieves and asserts the presence and content of the newly created issue. Dependencies include Playwright, JUnit, and Gson for JSON parsing.

```java
package org.example;

import com.google.gson.Gson;
import com.google.gson.JsonArray;
import com.google.gson.JsonElement;
import com.google.gson.JsonObject;
import com.microsoft.playwright.APIRequest;
import com.microsoft.playwright.APIRequestContext;
import com.microsoft.playwright.APIResponse;
import com.microsoft.playwright.Playwright;
import com.microsoft.playwright.options.RequestOptions;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.AfterAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.TestInstance;

import java.util.Collections;
import java.util.HashMap;
import java.util.Map;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;

@TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS)
public class TestGitHubAPI {
  private static final String REPO = "test-repo-2";
  private static final String USER = System.getenv("GITHUB_USER");
  private static final String API_TOKEN = System.getenv("GITHUB_API_TOKEN");

  private Playwright playwright;
  private APIRequestContext request;

  // ...

  @Test
  void shouldCreateBugReport() {
    Map<String, String> data = new HashMap<>();
    data.put("title", "[Bug] report 1");
    data.put("body", "Bug description");
    APIResponse newIssue = request.post("/repos/" + USER + "/" + REPO + "/issues",
      RequestOptions.create().setData(data));
    assertTrue(newIssue.ok());

    APIResponse issues = request.get("/repos/" + USER + "/" + REPO + "/issues");
    assertTrue(issues.ok());
    JsonArray json = new Gson().fromJson(issues.text(), JsonArray.class);
    JsonObject issue = null;
    for (JsonElement item : json) {
      JsonObject itemObj = item.getAsJsonObject();
      if (!itemObj.has("title")) {
        continue;
      }
      if ("[Bug] report 1".equals(itemObj.get("title").getAsString())) {
        issue = itemObj;
        break;
      }
    }
    assertNotNull(issue);
    assertEquals("Bug description", issue.get("body").getAsString(), issue.toString());
  }

  @Test
  void shouldCreateFeatureRequest() {
    Map<String, String> data = new HashMap<>();
    data.put("title", "[Feature] request 1");
    data.put("body", "Feature description");
    APIResponse newIssue = request.post("/repos/" + USER + "/" + REPO + "/issues",
      RequestOptions.create().setData(data));
    assertTrue(newIssue.ok());

    APIResponse issues = request.get("/repos/" + USER + "/" + REPO + "/issues");
    assertTrue(issues.ok());
    JsonArray json = new Gson().fromJson(issues.text(), JsonArray.class);
    JsonObject issue = null;
    for (JsonElement item : json) {
      JsonObject itemObj = item.getAsJsonObject();
      if (!itemObj.has("title")) {
        continue;
      }
      if ("[Feature] request 1".equals(itemObj.get("title").getAsString())) {
        issue = itemObj;
        break;
      }
    }
    assertNotNull(issue);
    assertEquals("Feature description", issue.get("body").getAsString(), issue.toString());
  }
}
```
